headline: Procter & Gamble vs. Colgate-Palmolive: One Dividend Giant Stands Above the Rest
summary: Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) and Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) both just reported, and the earnings reports sharpened a debate dividend investors have been having for years. P&G posted its fiscal Q3 2026 with core EPS of $1.59 on net sales of $21.235 billion. Colgate followed with Q1 2026 adjusted EPS of $0.97 on revenue of $5.324 billion. ... Procter & Gamble vs. Colgate-Palmolive: One Dividend Giant Stands Above the Rest

headline: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) Stock Weighed Against Cash Flow Value After Steady 2024 Run
summary: Wondering if Colgate-Palmolive is fairly priced or if the market is overlooking something at the current US$89.48 share price? This article focuses on what the numbers say about value rather than headlines. The stock has been relatively steady in the short term, with a 0.1% move over the last 7 days and a 0.6% decline over the last 30 days, while posting a 15.2% return year to date and a 4.0% return over the past year. headline: U.S.-Iran talks cancelled; oil on pace for weekly decline  - what’s moving markets
summary: Investing.com - U.S. stock futures edge lower after a strong rally on Wall Street driven by hopes that a U.S.-Iran peace deal could hold. Oil prices continue to retreat as traders bet the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz will help restore global crude supplies, although the abrupt cancellation of planned U.S.-Iran nuclear talks is raising questions about the durability of the agreement.

headline: Colgate Palmolive (CL) Stock Could Be 8% Undervalued on Its Margin Expansion Narrative
summary: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) is back in focus after recent share price moves and fresh performance data, giving investors a chance to reassess the consumer staples stock’s resilience and current valuation. See our latest analysis for Colgate-Palmolive. At a latest share price of $89.48, Colgate-Palmolive has seen a 15.18% year to date share price return. Its 1 year total shareholder return of 4.03% sits behind the stronger 3 and 5 year totals of 23.15% and 25.09%. This suggests momentum has been...
